name: gpu_conv3d
title: GPU 3D convolution
short_description: Three dimensional convolution throughput on the graphics processor.

structured_text.description: Runs repeated three dimensional convolutions over volume shaped data on the graphics processor, the operation used for video, medical imaging and volumetric models. Adding a third dimension makes the access pattern far heavier on memory than the two dimensional case, which tests a different limit of the hardware.
structured_text.metric_explanation: Throughput is the number of operations per second performed during the convolutions, in billions, reported in GFLOPS.
structured_text.value_interpretation: Higher is better. Strong 3D convolution throughput speeds up video understanding, volumetric medical analysis and other models that work over space and time.
structured_text.reasoning: Three dimensional convolution is much more demanding than the two dimensional version and is central to video and volumetric models, so it merits a separate, harder test.

domain_gaming.explanation: Games rarely use three dimensional convolution during play.

domain_office.explanation: Office work makes no use of volumetric convolution.

domain_software_development.explanation: Relevant to developers building video and volumetric models.

domain_graphics_design.explanation: Some volumetric and sequence effects use three dimensional convolution.

domain_video_editing.explanation: Video understanding models that span frames use three dimensional convolution.

domain_3d_rendering.explanation: Volumetric effects and simulation can use three dimensional convolution.

domain_audio_production.explanation: Audio work rarely uses volumetric convolution.

domain_cad_engineering.explanation: Volumetric inspection and simulation use three dimensional convolution.

domain_scientific_computing.explanation: Volumetric scientific data and stencil methods share this pattern.

domain_ai_training.explanation: Training video and volumetric models relies on three dimensional convolution.

domain_ai_inference.explanation: Running video and medical imaging models depends on this throughput.

domain_data_science.explanation: Analytics over volumetric and time series data uses three dimensional convolution.

domain_server_virt.explanation: Video and imaging inference services run this workload on shared GPUs.
